failure_modes.text: Bias blind spot and intellectual arrogance can make the current path feel safer simply because the team can defend it more fluently.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Bias Blind Spot and Intellectual Arrogance
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the default against a clean-sheet alternative: ask what the team would choose if starting from scratch, and explain any gap between that answer and the current path.

failure_modes.text: Illusion of knowing can make familiarity look like competence and suppress fresh investigation.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge fluency with fresh investigation: when defending the status quo feels easy, that comfort may be familiarity rather than evidence — require new data before recommitting.

failure_modes.text: Overoptimism can keep the default plan alive even when a conjunctive chain makes success much less likely than it feels.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the default plan's conjunctive assumptions: check whether staying the course requires too many things to go right simultaneously, and flag compounding risk.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What information am I currently ignoring or omitting because it contradicts my desired outcome or belief?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What information am I currently ignoring or omitting because it contradicts my desired outcome or belief?

premortem_questions.text: What would have to be true to accept an alternative strategy?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would have to be true to accept an alternative strategy?

heuristics.text: Use constructive confrontation to spell out the assumptions protecting the current default.
heuristics.source_quote: What Would You Have to Believe?

heuristics.text: Reduce choice overload with the less frame when decision paralysis is quietly preserving the default.
Use radical open-mindedness and perspective taking to pressure-test whether staying put is prudence or inertia.

curation_notes.summary: The raw file is rich on inertia-preserving failure content and explicit pre-mortem questions, so the chunk set stays close to those interventions.
donor_drops: Ignored donor topical examples that widened the model beyond incumbent-default testing and inertia pressure.
open_questions: The heuristics compress several related antidotes, but they remain tightly grounded in the raw countermeasures.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on cognitive dissonance, confirmation bias, and inside-view dynamics for later relation work.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is rich on first-principles thinking, design thinking, and radical open-mindedness.
structured_tensions: No explicit structured tension block is present, but the raw bias-preservation dynamics are strong enough for future relation preservation.
